summary: Remove the unconditional Accept: text/html,...,application/json;q=0.9 header from the shared do() function.
reason: The shared client is used for both HTML scrape paths and JSON v2 API endpoints. Setting an HTML-preferring Accept header globally would cause any content-negotiating API endpoint (/v2/foods/*, /api/services/reports/*) to return HTML and break JSON parsing. HTML scrape paths set their own Accept where it's actually the target; the Chrome TLS fingerprint (not the Accept header) is what clears MFP's anti-bot.
